title: Whitfieldia striata
intro: Whitfieldia striata, also known as Wild Mahogany or Thai Mahogany, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Whitfieldia striata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Whitfieldia striata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of traditional herbal remedies such as teas, capsules, and ointments.

Within the Acanthaceae family, Whitfieldia striata is classified in the genus Whitfieldia, which consists of several species of flowering plants.
